story_then_play.name: Story Then Play
story_then_play.description: Narrate the backstory, then begin playback.
story_then_play.instructions_text: Before playing the item, narrate its backstory using the actor's voice. Set the scene — context, intent, what makes this piece matter. Pause briefly to let the story land, then begin playback. After the item ends, allow a beat of silence before the next narration begins.

emotional_opening.name: Emotional Opening
emotional_opening.description: Lead with the most powerful moment or quote.
emotional_opening.instructions_text: Open with the most emotionally resonant line, quote, or moment from the item. Deliver it with weight and presence — let it land in silence before the actor introduces the piece. Then begin playback.

chapter_sequence.name: Chapter Sequence
chapter_sequence.description: Deliver section by section, in order.
chapter_sequence.instructions_text: Deliver the item in sequential order — one section or chapter at a time. Pause between sections to let each part breathe. Do not skip ahead or shuffle. The order is intentional.

late_night_reflection.name: Late Night Reflection
late_night_reflection.description: Quiet, contemplative pacing for an evening listen.
late_night_reflection.instructions_text: Set a quiet, contemplative tone. Speak softly. Slow the pacing. Leave space between thoughts. This is a late-night moment — the listener is winding down, reflective, present.

visual_first.name: Visual First
visual_first.description: Display visual before any audio plays.
visual_first.instructions_text: Before playing any audio, display the cover art or associated image. Let the visual set the tone. Pause for a moment, then begin playback.

quote_then_play.name: Quote Then Play
quote_then_play.description: Lead with a striking quote, then play.
quote_then_play.instructions_text: Find the most striking line or lyric from the item and present it first. Let it resonate, then begin playback. The quote is the hook; the content is the payoff.

full_immersion.name: Full Immersion
full_immersion.instructions_text: Play the item from start to finish with no interruptions. Do not pause, comment, or interject. Let the content speak for itself. This is a full immersion — the creator intended it to be experienced whole.

guided_walkthrough.name: Guided Walkthrough
guided_walkthrough.instructions_text: Walk the listener through the item section by section. Between each part, provide brief context — what to notice, what the creator intended, how it connects to what came before. Be a guide, not a lecturer. Keep commentary concise and warm.

compare_and_contrast.name: Compare & Contrast
compare_and_contrast.description: Highlight differences and connections across items.
compare_and_contrast.instructions_text: Present the item alongside related content in this experience. Highlight what makes each piece different — tone, style, message, era. Draw connections between them. Help the listener notice what they might miss alone.

loop_ambient.name: Loop Ambient
loop_ambient.instructions_text: Loop the item as ambient background. Do not introduce or narrate — just let it play on repeat. This content is meant to create atmosphere, not demand attention.

build_anticipation.name: Build Anticipation
build_anticipation.instructions_text: Build anticipation before revealing the item. Share pieces of the backstory without giving everything away. Tease with quotes or imagery. Let curiosity build. When the moment is right, begin playback.

inline_player.name: Inline Player
inline_player.description: Default — items render in-flow as cards.
inline_player.instructions_text: Present each item as an in-flow card with full surrounding chrome — controls, progress, item title, optional cover art. The default presentation; use for browsing-style experiences where listeners want context around each item.

album_art_forward.name: Album Art Forward
album_art_forward.description: Cover art is the hero, controls beneath.
album_art_forward.instructions_text: Present the cover art as the hero element of each item, with playback controls and metadata stacked beneath. Best for music or audio with strong visual identity.

performance_mode.name: Performance Mode
performance_mode.description: Fullscreen, listener-paced (tap to advance) — for live shows and installations.
performance_mode.instructions_text: Present items fullscreen with no UI chrome at all — no controls, no header, no progress indicator. Listener interacts via gestures (tap to reveal controls briefly, swipe to advance). Best for live-performance or installation contexts.

cinematic_fullscreen.name: Cinematic Fullscreen
cinematic_fullscreen.description: Fullscreen, autoplay through (muted start) — for films and lean-back viewing.
cinematic_fullscreen.instructions_text: Present each item full-bleed with no surrounding UI chrome. Where the content type supports it, autoplay muted to avoid surprising the listener. Maintain immersion — no UI distractions during playback.

background_visual.name: Background Visual
background_visual.description: Visual loops behind audio (Spotify Canvas style).
background_visual.instructions_text: For audio-primary items with an associated video or image sequence, loop the visual silently as a background while the audio plays. The visual is decoration — the audio is the focus. Listener can tap to mute the audio if desired.

letterbox_21_9.name: Letterbox 21:9
letterbox_21_9.instructions_text: Render video content in 21:9 cinemascope ratio with dark letterbox bars top and bottom. Best for cinematic video content where the creator authored in widescreen aspect.

text_overlay.name: Text Overlay
text_overlay.description: Display synced text (lyrics, transcript, captions) during playback.
text_overlay.instructions_text: Render synced text as a scrolling overlay during playback — lyrics for songs, transcripts for podcasts and lectures, captions for film, or script text for narration. The active line is highlighted as playback advances. Use whichever timed-text field is populated on the content (lrc_lyrics for songs, lrc_data for other types) to keep the overlay in sync.

document_excerpt.name: Document Excerpt
document_excerpt.description: First ~200 words inline with expand affordance.
document_excerpt.instructions_text: For document-type content, render the first ~200 words inline with an "Read more" affordance that opens the full reader. Lets listeners get a taste without committing to a full read.

image_sequence.name: Image Sequence
image_sequence.instructions_text: For a sequence of image-type items, dwell on each image for a set number of seconds before auto-advancing to the next. The default dwell is 5 seconds; engines may make this configurable per-item via metadata.

cross_fade_transitions.name: Cross-fade Transitions
cross_fade_transitions.instructions_text: Cross-fade between items rather than hard-cutting. Audio cross-fades for last 2 seconds; visual cross-fades for full transition duration. Smoother flow at the cost of a beat of overlapping content.

web_page.name: Web Page
web_page.description: Simpler web-page presentation. Items render in-flow with clear section headers, generous spacing, readable typography. The opt-out from broadcast rendering — no cold open, no chyrons, no sign-off.
web_page.instructions_text: Present the experience as a standard web page. Items render in schema order as in-flow cards with clear section headers above each collection wrapper and generous breathing room between items. Typography is clean and readable; controls are obvious; visual scenes provide quiet backdrops. Do not impose editorial structure beyond what the experience schema describes — let the items and their intro_hints carry the experience. No cold open, no chyrons, no sign-off — just a well-designed page. Use this framing when the content does not suit a broadcast format (reference material, galleries, document-heavy experiences).

broadcast_show.name: Broadcast Show
broadcast_show.description: TV-program feel: cold open, segment title cards, chyrons, host voiceover, sign-off. Best for albums, films, podcasts, narrative experiences.
broadcast_show.instructions_text:
Present the `experience` as a broadcast TV program you are Directing. ROLE BINDING (every editorial word in this brief maps to a schema key — read this once, apply throughout): - The creator (the platform user, owner of the `experience`) is the DIRECTOR of the show. - The schema's `actor` plays the role of HOST / PRESENTER. (`resolved_actor` is the per-item resolved value when an override exists.) Wherever this brief uses the word "host", it refers to `actor`. Voice / prose style → `actor.narrative_voice`. Persona type → `actor.actor_type`. Display name → `actor.name`. - IMPORTANT — `actor.visual_style` and `actor.visual_directives` are SUBJECT-IN-IMAGE directives. They describe what should/shouldn't appear *inside* generated images of the actor (no faces, no logos, muted colors, etc.) — they're consumed by image-generation pipelines, NOT by the page renderer. Do not interpret `actor.visual_directives` items like `no_text_overlays`, `no_ui_elements`, or `no_logos` as page-level instructions. Page rendering is controlled by per-item `display_instructions` (and the typed `display_directives` slug list) on each content row — see THREE-LAYER CASCADE and OVERLAYS below. - Collections (each item with `collection_id`) are SEGMENT BLOCKS — season / chapter / episode / anthology, per `collection_type`. - Content items (each `collection_content[]` entry, or each top-level item with `content_id`) are SEGMENT CLIPS. - Show title → `experience.name`. Show cover art → `experience.cover_art_url`. Show one-line premise → `experience.description`. Show-ident bug text → `experience.name` rendered small-caps subdued. CRITICAL — YOU ARE WRITING AS THE ACTOR. Every narration block (cold-open monologue, segment intros, clip throws, sign-off) is COMPOSED prose in the style described by `actor.narrative_voice`. Do NOT echo `intro_hint` (experience / collection level) or `outro_hint` strings verbatim as page copy — that produces a printed rundown sheet, not a hosted broadcast. Use `intro_hint`, `delivery_instructions[]`, `metadata.primary_quote`, `metadata.full_story`, `mood_tags`, and `collection_date_range` as INPUTS to compose fresh prose. See NARRATION SEQUENCING in the wrapper below for full rules and a worked example. COLD OPEN - The first scene is a cold-open card showing `experience.cover_art_url`, `experience.name` as the title, `experience.description` as the one-line premise, and the actor's `actor.name` (or the `profile_name` of the experience if more appropriate) as the on-screen credit. The first three seconds should feel like a program is about to air. - Follow the cold open with the actor's voiceover — COMPOSE a 2–4 sentence cold-open monologue in the style described by `actor.narrative_voice`, using `experience.intro_hint` as the seed and `experience.description` for grounding. Render as

block(s). Do not paste `intro_hint` verbatim. - Include a persistent show-ident bug in a corner of the page: `experience.name` in small caps, subdued. It carries across scene changes. RUNDOWN - Air every `items[]` entry in `sort_order`. A broadcast doesn't shuffle its rundown. - Each collection-type item becomes a SEGMENT TITLE CARD scene. Hold the card for a beat before the first nested clip rolls. Format the card label using `collection_type` + `collection_numeral` + `collection_name` + `collection_date_range`. Examples: "Chapter I: Innocence & Heartbreak · 1998–2000" for `collection_type === 'chapter'`, "Season 2: The Doubt Sessions" for `'season'`, "Episode 3: Late Night Reflections" for `'episode'`. If `collection_type` doesn't map cleanly, fall back to `collection_name` + `collection_numeral`. PRESENTER SCRIPT (every slot is one of two paths — VERBATIM if the field's key is in the parent entity's `tts_fields` opt-in array; COMPOSED otherwise. Verbatim slots also have pre-rendered audio cached. See NARRATION SEQUENCING in the master prompt for the full contract — this section just spells out which fields land where.) - Cold-open monologue at the top: if `intro_hint` is in `experience.tts_fields` → speak `experience.intro_hint` VERBATIM. Otherwise → COMPOSE 2–4 sentences from `experience.intro_hint` (as direction) + `experience.description` in `actor.narrative_voice`. - Before each segment block: if `intro_hint` is in the collection's `tts_fields` → speak `collection_metadata.intro_hint` VERBATIM. Otherwise → COMPOSE 1–3 sentences from `collection_metadata.intro_hint` (as direction) + `collection_metadata.mood_tags` + `collection_date_range` in `actor.narrative_voice`. - Before each clip rolls, the throw is one of two paths: • VERBATIM — if `intro_hint` is in the junction row's `tts_fields` (the per-row `intro_hint` field on `experience_items` / `content_collections` — labeled "Intro Hint" in the dashboard), speak that exact text. Do NOT paraphrase or expand. Do NOT compose around it. • COMPOSED — otherwise, COMPOSE a clip throw 2–4 sentences in `actor.narrative_voice` (or `resolved_actor.narrative_voice` if a per-item override exists). When the row's `intro_hint` has text but is NOT opted into `tts_fields`, treat that text as direction alongside the structured seeds. Structured seeds: `delivery_instructions[]`, `metadata.full_story`, `metadata.short_description`, `metadata.primary_quote`, `arc_role`, `mood_tags`, year fragment from `metadata`. `delivery_instructions[]` are the pacing rules for HOW the throw lands (e.g. "share background story in a reflective tone, pause briefly, then begin playback" → actor shares the backstory, lets a beat pass, then says "Here's [title]"). - Per-item actor override: if `resolved_actor` is present on a clip and differs from the experience-level `actor`, use `resolved_actor.narrative_voice` as the style for that clip ONLY. - Never repeat a segment intro for every clip inside it. - Variety matters: every clip-throw should be specific to that clip (year, place, the line, the moment). Identical-feeling throws break the broadcast. CHYRONS & LOWER-THIRDS - When a clip is playing, display a lower-third overlay: clip `title`, `content_type_name` as a badge, year from `metadata` if present. Bottom-left, subdued — broadcast-style "NAME · TITLE" banner. - Segment title cards display full-width, held for a beat. STAGE DESIGN — ROLLING BROADCAST (not a scroll page, NOT a slideshow) A real broadcast plays itself. The viewer sits back and watches; they don't click "Next" between every segment. The show ROLLS — segment title cards hold for a beat then transition into the first clip; when a clip's audio ends, the show advances to the next scene automatically; coming-soon teaser scenes hold for a few seconds then roll forward. Render the experience as ONE full-viewport scene at a time, with the show advancing itself. REQUIRED SCENE SEQUENCE (each scene fills the viewport): 1. Cold-open scene (`experience.cover_art_url` + `experience.name` + `experience.description` + composed actor monologue). Hold ~4-5 seconds after the monologue lays out, then auto-advance. 2. For each collection-type item: a Segment Title Card scene (`collection_type` + `collection_numeral` + `collection_name` + `collection_date_range` + composed segment intro), held ~3-4 seconds, then auto-advance into the first entry in `collection_content[]`. 3. For each `collection_content[]` entry: a Clip scene. While audio is playing, the show stays on this scene. When audio ends (or for non-playable items, after a held read interval), auto-advance. 4. For each standalone content-type item (no `collection_id`): a Clip scene with the same auto-advance behavior. 5. Sign-off scene (composed actor outro + `coming_soon` teaser referencing those clips by `title`). Holds; no auto-advance from here. CLIP SCENE COMPOSITION - One clip at a time: `cover_art_url`, the throw (verbatim junction script if set, otherwise the composed clip throw in the actor's narrative voice — see NARRATION SEQUENCING wrapper section for the two-path rule), lower-third overlay (`title` + `content_type_name` badge + year), media controls bound to `media_play_url`, `metadata.primary_quote` rendered as a pull-quote. - TEXT OVERLAY: when the resolved `display_instructions` includes the text-overlay instruction (the `text_overlay` recipe; instructions_text starts with "Render synced text as a scrolling overlay…") AND the clip's `metadata.lyrics` is non-empty, render the lyrics as a side-column scrolling overlay during playback. Songs in this catalog carry full `metadata.lyrics` per-clip; \n is the real line break. Active line at full opacity, surrounding lines at 60-70%. Skip silently if metadata.lyrics is empty. - Previous and next clips are NOT visible. - Coming-soon clips (`status === 'coming_soon'`): NO audio embedded, held ~5-7 seconds (long enough to read the throw + the short_description + the primary_quote), then auto-advance. (No text overlay either — there's no playback to sync against.) - THE HOST SPEAKS the throw. Whether the throw is a verbatim junction script or composed prose, render it as audio via the NARRATION AUDIO chain (Tier 1 pre-generated URL → Tier 2 Web Speech → Tier 3 silent text). See NARRATION AUDIO in the wrapper section below for the full chain. AUTO-ADVANCE BEHAVIOR - Active audio clips: when `ended` event fires on the audio element, auto-advance to the next scene. - Non-audio held scenes (cold open, segment title cards, coming-soon clips, sign-off-not): hold for the durations above (3-7 seconds depending on density), then auto-advance. - User can pause auto-advance: clicking Pause on a clip's audio pauses auto-advance for that scene. The show waits for the user to resume (or to manually advance). - User agency preserved: hovering over the viewport reveals the navigation chrome; mouse-idle for ~3 seconds fades the chrome out so it doesn't compete with the scene visually. TRANSITIONS BETWEEN SCENES - CROSS-FADE / soft dissolve, ~1.2-1.8 second duration. - NOT slide-in animations (`translateX`). NOT hard cuts. - The `visual_scene` backdrop crossfades alongside the scene swap (`color_palette` accent fades through, motion continues across the transition rather than restarting). NAVIGATION CHROME (quiet, not the dominant UI) - Provide Next/Prev controls + arrow-key handlers + space-to-advance for power users who want to skip ahead or replay. - Make them QUIET: subtle bottom-edge controls that auto-fade-out after ~3 seconds of mouse idleness, fade in on mouse movement. Not chunky outlined buttons sitting in the corner. - Show a subtle progress indicator (e.g., a thin bottom-edge line filling left-to-right across the experience), NOT a prominent "Scene 5 of 18" counter. - No big "End" CTA at the last scene — the sign-off is the end card. CONTINUITY - The show-ident bug (`experience.name` rendered subdued in a corner) persists across all scenes as a fixed-position overlay. - The lower-third on Clip scenes appears when audio starts, persists during playback, fades out on transition. - Hold one `visual_scene` per segment unless a clip provides its own `visual_scene` — see USING THE VISUAL SCENE OBJECT below for how to consume every field. ANTI-SLIDESHOW CHECKLIST (avoid these slideshow tropes) - ❌ `translateX` slide-in transitions → use cross-fade - ❌ Prominent "Scene N of M" indicator → use a subtle bottom-edge progress line - ❌ Big chunky Next/Prev buttons sitting in the corner → quiet auto-fading edge controls - ❌ "End" or "Done" CTA at the last scene → just hold on the sign-off - ❌ Every scene waiting for a click → auto-advance per the durations above - ❌ Static scenes once active → `visual_scene` motion (drift / pulse) continues; subtle fade-in animations on text SIGN-OFF - After the last clip, deliver the outro. If `outro_hint` is in `experience.tts_fields` → speak `experience.outro_hint` VERBATIM. Otherwise → COMPOSE 2–4 sentences in the style described by `actor.narrative_voice`: a "thanks for watching"-style line, `experience.name`, the actor credit (`actor.name`), with `experience.outro_hint` (if set) as direction. If any items have `status === 'coming_soon'`, add a brief "coming up on a future episode" teaser referencing those clips by `title`. - No abrupt end. Sign off like a broadcast.

broadcast_station_ident.name: Broadcast Show — Station ID
broadcast_station_ident.description: TV-program feel with a brand bumper instead of a cold open. Brief station identity opens the show, then segment cards, chyrons, host voiceover, and sign-off. Best for branded networks, channel-style experiences, multi-show creators.
broadcast_station_ident.instructions_text:
Present the `experience` as a broadcast TV program you are Directing — same shell as the `broadcast_show` framing, except the opening is a Station Identity bumper (a brief brand intro), NOT a cold-open monologue.

ROLE BINDING (every editorial word in this brief maps to a schema key — read this once, apply throughout):

- The creator (the platform user, owner of the `experience`) is the DIRECTOR of the show.
- The schema's `actor` plays the role of HOST / PRESENTER. (`resolved_actor` is the per-item resolved value when an override exists.) Wherever this brief uses the word "host", it refers to `actor`. Voice / prose style → `actor.narrative_voice`. Persona type → `actor.actor_type`. Display name → `actor.name`.
- IMPORTANT — `actor.visual_style` and `actor.visual_directives` are SUBJECT-IN-IMAGE directives. They describe what should/shouldn't appear *inside* generated images of the actor (no faces, no logos, muted colors, etc.) — they're consumed by image-generation pipelines, NOT by the page renderer. Do not interpret `actor.visual_directives` items like `no_text_overlays`, `no_ui_elements`, or `no_logos` as page-level instructions. Page rendering is controlled by per-item `display_instructions` (and the typed `display_directives` slug list) on each content row — see THREE-LAYER CASCADE and OVERLAYS in the broadcast_show wrapper.
- Collections (each item with `collection_id`) are SEGMENT BLOCKS — season / chapter / episode / anthology, per `collection_type`.
- Content items (each `collection_content[]` entry, or each top-level item with `content_id`) are SEGMENT CLIPS.
- Show title → `experience.name`. Show cover art → `experience.cover_art_url`. Show one-line premise → `experience.description`. Show-ident bug text → `experience.name` rendered small-caps subdued.

CRITICAL — YOU ARE WRITING AS THE ACTOR. Every narration block (segment intros, clip throws, sign-off) is COMPOSED prose in the style described by `actor.narrative_voice`. The Station ID bumper is the ONLY exception — see STATION IDENTITY below.

STATION IDENTITY (replaces the cold open)
- The first scene is a brief BRAND BUMPER, not a host monologue. ~5-10 seconds total.
- Render the bumper card with: `experience.station_logo_url` (the dedicated visual brand mark — typically a wordmark, emblem, or short animated logo) as the primary held visual element, `experience.name` rendered as a network/show ident in small caps, and the `experience.station_ident` line as the spoken brand line. `station_logo_url` is Pro-gated, so when null (Free tier or unset) the renderer falls back to the platform's default bumper mark — type-only renderers can fall further back to `experience.cover_art_url`, and finally to the show ident in type alone.
- The HOST speaks ONE short line composed from `experience.station_ident` as the seed. It should sound like a network ID — branded, punchy, premise-light. Examples in the style of `actor.narrative_voice`:
  • Seed: "This is Wave Radio." → spoken: "This is Wave Radio. Stay tuned."
  • Seed: "You're listening to The Late Show." → spoken: "You're listening to The Late Show — back in a moment."
  • Seed: "Channel 7 News." → spoken: "Channel 7 News. The stories of your week, on the way."
- Compose a SINGLE short sentence (or two extremely short sentences). Do NOT echo `station_ident` verbatim if the seed is a fragment — flesh it into a natural broadcast ident. Do NOT expand into a cold-open-style monologue.
- If `experience.station_ident` is empty/null: fall back to a generic ident composed from `experience.name` ("This is [name]." / "You're with [name]."). Still ~5-10 seconds, still one short line.
- After the bumper holds and the host line lands, transition directly into the rundown — NO separate cold-open scene, NO host monologue introducing the show.
- Include a persistent show-ident bug in a corner of the page: `experience.name` in small caps, subdued. It carries across scene changes (same as broadcast_show).

RUNDOWN, PRESENTER SCRIPT, CHYRONS, CLIP SCENES, AUTO-ADVANCE, TRANSITIONS, NAVIGATION CHROME, CONTINUITY, ANTI-SLIDESHOW CHECKLIST, SIGN-OFF
- Identical to the `broadcast_show` framing recipe. Compose segment intros, clip throws, and the sign-off in the actor's narrative voice. Render lower-thirds. Auto-advance. Cross-fade transitions. Quiet navigation chrome. Persistent show-ident bug. Sign off (composed outro using `experience.outro_hint` as a seed) — no abrupt end.

REQUIRED SCENE SEQUENCE
  1. Station Identity bumper scene (cover/brand mark + `experience.name` ident + composed short brand line from `experience.station_ident`). Hold ~5-7 seconds total, then auto-advance.
  2. For each collection-type item: a Segment Title Card scene (same as broadcast_show).
  3. For each `collection_content[]` entry: a Clip scene with auto-advance (same as broadcast_show).
  4. For each standalone content-type item: a Clip scene (same as broadcast_show).
  5. Sign-off scene (composed actor outro + `coming_soon` teaser referencing those clips by `title`). Holds; no auto-advance from here.

The rest of the contract — clip-scene composition, narration audio chain, visual scene consumption, three-layer cascade, overlays, narration sequencing — is identical to `broadcast_show`. Apply that wrapper guidance unchanged. The ONLY difference is the opening: a brief Station Identity bumper instead of a cold-open scene + host monologue.
